ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-04-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for and with Richard Layne (Trustee).

The Court expressed concern about how quickly the Trust is being spent and also stated that spending has improved since the last accounting period. Mr. Senick and Mr. Layne addressed the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes and presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court granted the Third Account (subject to review of the order). The Court will determine if a one or a two year account period will be required.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-21-2012 Continued To Jun-04-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-02-2012 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for and with Richard Layne (Trustee).

Mr. Senick stated that all expenses dropped significantly since the last account period, that it is at the discretion of the Trustee as to how to spend the money and that the Veterinarian bill is appropriate because the Pet is important to the Beneficiary. The Court stated that the Special Needs Trust needs to be preserved, that expenditures need to be sensible and reminded the Trustee of his Fiduciary duties. The Court approved the Second Account (subject to review and modificaion of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-28-2010 Continued To Jul-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for Richard Layne (Trustee).

Mr. Senick explained that the estimated amount of the Special Needs Trust was based on the sale of the property and that the property sold for less than anticipated, that the $15,000.00 had been in the Mother's Trust prior to the sale of the property and that there never was any pay out from Social Security. In addition, Mr. Senick stated that he felt that the Special Needs Trust should be used for anything needed by the Beneficiary that was not provided by Medical and Social Security. Mr. Layne stated that he holds access to the debit card, that the debit card was used when the Beneficiary saw something that he wanted, that they sometimes went together to purchase those items and that sometimes the Beneficiary went on his own, and that he got receipts for all purchases. The matter was continued to July 19, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Senick is to file a declaration addressing the discrepancy in the order establishing the Special Needs Trust and the origin of the funds. Mr. Layne is to file a declaration addressing the debit card which is to be attached to the debit card statements. There will be no use of the debit card going forward. Said declarations are to be filed by July 13, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-10-2010 Off Calendar

Attorney-In-Fact'S Petition For Order Authorizing Id Soecial Needs Trust
Off Calendar Feb-19-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Allen Senick for and with Petitioner Richard Layne, Trustor Eugene N. Barcelos present in pro per.

The Court requested proof of the disability of Trustor, in which Mr. Senick presented a dated copy of the document. Mr. Senick stated that he will file a year-end summary supplemental declaration tomorrow in support of such proof of receiving benefits of social security payments. The Court confirmed with Mr. Barcelos on the record that he understood and consented to each of the Trust provisions with informed consent of the waiver of Bond, Accounting, payment of attorney fees and costs, and Successor Trustee Sandra Ribiero.
The Court granted the Petition, Order submitted in court but the Court will not sign the Order until the supplemental declaration is filed and submitted.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Sajja Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.
